task_completion.visual_evidence: The Docker Desktop application is open and the Settings (gear icon) window is visible.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Docker Engine configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Docker Engine' tab is selected in the left sidebar of the Docker Desktop settings window.
The JSON configuration editor in the Docker Engine settings shows the 'bip' key added to the JSON object.

task_completion.description: Set the 'bip' value to the required subnet gateway
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'bip' key in the JSON editor is assigned the exact string value '192.168.200.1/24'.
The 'Apply & restart' button is clicked, or there is a visual indicator that Docker Desktop is restarting/applying changes.

task_completion.description: Verify the engine reflects the new bridge subnet
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows the output of a network inspection (e.g., 'docker network inspect bridge'), clearly displaying the Subnet as '192.168.200.0/24' and Gateway as '192.168.200.1'.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual Docker Desktop application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the genuine Docker Desktop GUI being used to edit the configuration, rather than a generic text editor mocking the file.

integrity.description: Agent did not spoof the verification output
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal output verifying the network configuration comes from a genuine Docker command (like 'docker network inspect bridge'), not from an 'echo' command printing the expected text.
The edited JSON in the Docker Engine configuration has proper formatting (quotes, commas, braces) and does not show any red syntax error highlights that would prevent Docker from starting.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected subnet for the default bridge network is '192.168.200.0/24'. The expected gateway is '192.168.200.1'. The required configuration key to set in the Docker Engine JSON is 'bip', which must be set to '192.168.200.1/24'.
